directories.purpose: Express route handlers — one file per feature domain (auth.ts, dashboard.ts, settings.ts)
Business logic layer — pure functions with no Express dependency, independently testable
React component tree — organized by feature with shared UI components in src/components/ui/
Express middleware — JWT validation, rate limiting, request logging, error handling

key_files.purpose: Canonical database schema — all entities, fields, indexes, and relations defined here
JWT validation middleware applied to all protected routes — extracts and verifies user identity
SendGrid client wrapper — single integration point for all transactional email sending

libraries.name: jsonwebtoken
libraries.purpose: JWT signing and verification for stateless authentication sessions

libraries.name: bcrypt
libraries.purpose: Password hashing with configurable cost factor — used at registration and password change

libraries.name: zod
libraries.purpose: Runtime schema validation for all API request bodies — prevents invalid data from reaching services

libraries.name: pino
libraries.purpose: Structured JSON logging with low overhead — replaces console.log across all backend modules

entities.name: User

fields.name: password_hash
fields.constraints: NOT NULL, bcrypt hash stored — never plaintext

indexes: idx_users_email (email) — used on every login and registration check

entities.name: UserSettings

fields.name: user_id
fields.constraints: FOREIGN KEY -> User.id, ON DELETE CASCADE

indexes: idx_user_settings_user_id (user_id) — used on every settings read/write

relationships.description: Each user has exactly one settings record created on registration
A user may have multiple pending reset tokens but only the latest is valid

data_model.migration_notes: Migrations run via prisma migrate deploy in CI before application startup. All migrations are idempotent — safe to re-run if deployment fails mid-way.

components.name: AuthService
components.responsibility: Handles all authentication business logic — registration, login validation, password hashing, JWT issuance, and reset token lifecycle

components.name: DashboardService
components.responsibility: Aggregates project metrics, activity feed items, and widget configuration for the authenticated user's dashboard view

components.name: Dashboard
components.responsibility: Primary UI component rendering the authenticated user's dashboard with configurable widget grid, real-time data, and empty state handling
dependencies: SWR for data fetching with revalidation

design_decisions.decision: Use Prisma ORM over raw SQL for all database operations
design_decisions.context: Team needs type-safe database access without writing verbose SQL; migration management must be version-controlled
alternatives_considered: Raw pg driver with hand-written SQL
design_decisions.rationale: Prisma schema is the single source of truth for both TypeScript types and database structure — eliminates class of bugs where code and DB drift apart. Migration workflow is simpler than TypeORM's ActiveRecord pattern.
consequences: Prisma client must be regenerated after every schema change (handled in CI)
Complex queries require raw SQL escape hatch via prisma.$queryRaw

design_decisions.decision: Store widget layout as JSON in PostgreSQL rather than normalized rows
design_decisions.context: Dashboard widget configuration is user-specific and has no cross-user query requirements
alternatives_considered: Normalized widget_preferences table with one row per widget
Separate Redis store for ephemeral layout state
design_decisions.rationale: Layout is read and written as a complete unit — normalization would require assembling the grid from multiple rows on every dashboard load with no query benefit. JSON column with Prisma is type-safe and simpler.
consequences: Layout validation happens in application layer (Zod schema)
Cannot query individual widget settings across users — acceptable given no cross-user reporting requirement
